name: Click
what_it_does: Python command-line interface creation toolkit — composable CLI framework using decorators. Click features: @click.command(), @click.option() with type validation, @click.argument(), @click.group() for subcommands, automatic --help generation, parameter types (INT, FLOAT, BOOL, Choice, Path, File, DateTime), prompts (@click.password_option), confirmation dialogs, progress bars (click.progressbar()), colored output (click.style, click.echo), context (click.pass_context), testing utilities (CliRunner), and multi-command composition. Used by Flask CLI, dbt, Black, and many Python agent CLI tools.
use_cases: Agent CLI tool — @click.group(); @cli.command(); @click.option('--agent-id', required=True, type=int); @click.option('--verbose', is_flag=True); def run(agent_id, verbose): agent.run(agent_id) — full CLI with help, validation, and flags for agent management tools

Agent admin commands — @cli.command(); @click.argument('agent_type', type=click.Choice(['search', 'code', 'data'])); @click.option('--count', default=1, show_default=True) — validated agent type argument with choice validation; agents deploy with typed CLI args

Agent interactive prompts — @click.option('--api-key', prompt='OpenAI API Key', hide_input=True, confirmation_prompt=True) — secure password-style prompt for agent API key configuration; click handles masked input and confirmation

Agent CLI progress display — with click.progressbar(agent_tasks, label='Processing') as bar: for task in bar: process(task) — built-in progress bar for batch agent task processing; automatic ETA calculation

Agent CLI testing — from click.testing import CliRunner; runner = CliRunner(); result = runner.invoke(cli, ['run', '--agent-id', '1']); assert result.exit_code == 0 — full CLI integration testing without subprocess; agent CLI tests run in-process
not_for: TUI/interactive apps — Click is for CLI commands; for terminal user interfaces use Textual or urwid
Argument parsing only — if you only need argument parsing without decorators, argparse is stdlib alternative; Click's decorator system is overhead for simple script args
Sub-100ms CLI startup — Click imports add ~50ms startup; for ultra-fast CLI tools where startup time matters use argparse or custom solution
best_when: Building Python agent CLI tools, admin commands, or management interfaces — Click's decorator API makes complex CLIs with subcommands, validation, and testing straightforward.
avoid_when: You need a TUI interface, have startup time constraints, or prefer stdlib argparse for minimal dependencies.

auth.notes: No auth — local CLI framework. Agent API keys managed via prompt options or environment variables.

pricing.notes: Click is BSD-3-Clause licensed, maintained by the Pallets project. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local CLI library. ~50ms startup overhead from Click import. CliRunner for testing runs in-process with zero subprocess overhead.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Click shows usage error with 'Error: Invalid value for --option: expected TYPE' messages. Missing required options show 'Error: Missing option --option'. Invalid Choice shows list of valid options. --help auto-generated from docstrings and option help= strings. CliRunner captures stdout/stderr for test assertion.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Click decorators are idempotent — same CLI definition registers same command. CliRunner invocations are idempotent for the same args. Command execution idempotency depends on the command implementation.
known_agent_gotchas: @click.pass_context required for context access — ctx = click.get_current_context() works but @click.pass_context + def cmd(ctx) is idiomatic; agent CLI subcommands needing shared state (like db connection) should store in ctx.obj = {}; forgetting pass_context means context is unavailable in command function

click.echo vs print for agent CLIs — click.echo handles encoding correctly on all platforms; print() may fail on Windows with non-ASCII agent output; use click.echo() for all agent CLI output; click.secho() adds style; click.echo(err=True) writes to stderr

CliRunner isolation may hide real failures — runner.invoke(cli, args, catch_exceptions=False) re-raises exceptions; default catch_exceptions=True silently catches agent command exceptions; agent CLI tests should use catch_exceptions=False to surface real failures not just check exit code

Option ordering matters for @click.argument — arguments are positional; multiple arguments defined in wrong order cause confusing CLI behavior; agent CLIs should prefer options (--flag value) over positional arguments for clarity and optional parameters

invoke_without_command needed for group defaults — @click.group(invoke_without_command=True) allows group without subcommand; without it, calling group name without subcommand prints help; agent CLI tools wanting 'agent' to default to 'agent status' need invoke_without_command=True + ctx.invoked_subcommand check

Windows terminal color support requires colorama — click.style() colored output works on macOS/Linux terminals; Windows requires colorama package; agent CLI tools with colored status output fail on Windows CI without colorama; add colorama to dependencies or check sys.platform before styling

security_score_components.security_notes: Use hide_input=True for agent API keys and passwords in Click prompts. Never pass secrets as positional arguments (visible in ps output and shell history). Prefer environment variables (envvar='AGENT_API_KEY' in click.option) over CLI arguments for agent credentials.
